title: High-throughput Hisx6-tagged protein purification
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"5j0vi","text":"We have developed a high-throughput protein purification protocol that allows us to purify ~4,000 proteins within 10 hours from prepared culture.  By combining steps of cell lysis and protein capture on affinity resins in sealed filter plates, we reduced the number of pipetting steps and thus, human errors.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Re-suspend cell pellets at 4 °C in 80 µL of lysis buffer, containing 50 mM NaH 2 PO 4 at pH 8 with 300 mM NaCl, 20 mM imidazole, CelLytic B (Sigma), Lysozyme (1 mg/mL), Benzonase (50 units/mL), proteinase inhibitor cocktail (Sigma), and PMSF (1 mM/mL).
Wash Ni-NTA Superflow (QIAGEN) twice with water and twice with 50 mM NaH 2 PO 4 at pH 8 with 300 mM NaCl, 20 mM imidazole using centrifugation.
Add 25 μL pre-washed Ni-NTA Superflow (QIAGEN) to each well
Transfer the mixtures into bottom-sealed filter plates (Multiscreen Nylon Mesh)
Incubated for 1.5 hr at 4 °C with vigorously shaking

Wash the resin-protein complexes 3 times with 250 µL/well of wash buffer I (50 mM NaH 2 PO 4 with 300 mM NaCl, 10% glycerol, 20 mM imidazole, 0.01% Triton X-100, at pH 8) and 3 times with Wash buffer II (50 mM NaH 2 PO 4 with 150 mM NaCl, 25% glycerol, 20 mM imidazole, 0.01% Triton X-100, at pH 8) using a Q-fill2 (Gentix, UK).
Add 25 μL of elution buffer (50 mM NaH 2 PO 4 /150 mM NaCl/25% glycerol/250 mM imidazole/0.01% Triton X-100, pH 7.5)
Elute proteins into 96-well receiver plates by centrifugation.
Store the proteins in 96-well plates at -80 °C

A proteome chip approach reveals new DNA damage recognition activities in Escherichia coli by Chien-Sheng Chen, Ekaterina Korobkova, Hao Chen, Jian Zhu, Xing Jian, Sheng-Ce Tao, Chuan He & Heng Zhu published in Nature Methods on 07 Dec, 2007 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nmeth1148
creator: Protocols Importer
